channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: War powers. Attorney general. Jan. 6 video. California primary. ‘60 Minutes.’ Heat wave. Manhole mystery. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Omega-3 fats are brain boosters. Here are 7 ways to eat more of them.
item.description: Plant foods are an excellent source of omega-3 fats. These recipes for salads, snacks and more can help you add them to your diet.

item.title: This ‘necessary and proper’ ban could almost drive someone to drink
item.description: This ‘necessary and proper’ ban could almost drive someone to drink

item.title: In a first, House votes to block Trump from ordering further strikes on Iran
item.description: The Senate advanced a similar war powers resolution last month on a procedural vote, reflecting growing impatience with a conflict Congress hasn’t authorized.

item.title: A voter’s guide to the 2026 D.C. primary election
item.description: What to know as D.C. voters head to the polls on June 16 to pick Democratic nominees for several key races including mayor and the delegate to the House.

item.title: U.S., intelligence allies issue rare warning that China is using LinkedIn to target military officers, spies for secret information
item.description: Officials said it marked the first time that members of the Five Eyes intelligence-sharing group — the United States, United Kingdom, Canada, Australia and New Zealand — combined to issue a public warning about the expanding threat over networking platforms.

item.title: What Tuesday told MAGA and the Democratic establishment
item.description: Trump lost one, Democrats dodged some: Here's what the primary results revealed.

item.title: Bari Weiss says fired ‘60 Minutes’ correspondent Scott Pelley violated ‘trust and mutual respect’
item.description: The CBS News editor in chief says she sought to “find a way back” with Pelley before his firing, according to an audio recording obtained by The Washington Post, a claim he disputed Wednesday.

item.title: I try!! But can’t pronounce brother’s boyfriend’s name. Hax readers give advice.
item.description: Letter writer can’t even hear the subtle vowel shift, much less reproduce it as speech, and it’s straining a valued relationship.

item.title: What to do when someone’s perfume is overbearing in a packed restaurant? Ask your dining questions.
item.description: Washington Post food critic Elazar Sontag entertains your restaurant questions, rants and raves.

item.title: Trump diverts millions from national park visitor fees for July 4 celebration, D.C. fountains
item.description: That money is being spent in Washington at the expense of the rest of the nation’s park system, advocates say, which has a $24 billion backlog of badly needed infrastructure repairs and improvements, known as “deferred maintenance.”

item.title: This person’s replacement is the topic of today’s On the Record
item.description: This person’s replacement is the topic of today’s On the Record

item.title: Trump library says no Twitter DMs can be found, despite evidence he sent them
item.description: The newly operational Trump Presidential Library, the entity responsible for preserving records from the White House, says that it cannot find a single Twitter direct message sent by a president who tweeted more than 25,000 times during his first administration.

item.title: Key primary takeaways from a big night in Iowa, California and more
item.description:
In Iowa, Josh Turek won the Democratic nomination for Senate, while Trump-endorsed Randy Feenstra conceded defeat in the GOP governor’s primary.
Republican Steve Hilton, a former Fox News host, and Democrat Xavier Becerra, a former health secretary, were leading in the California gubernatorial primary, which remained uncalled.

item.title: Primary results. Payout fund. Trump’s arch. Jan. 6 rioter. Scott Pelley. GLP-1s and cancer. Super ager. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Trump’s pick for Iowa governor loses GOP primary, in blow to president’s dominance among Republicans
item.description: President Donald Trump had backed Rep. Randy Feenstra last week, calling him “MAGA all the way.” Although the congressman was better known than his rivals, he lost to Zach Lahn, a farmer who had already drawn the support of some conservative activists critical of Feenstra and surged in polling toward the end of the race.

item.title: State Rep. Josh Turek, a favorite of the Democratic establishment, wins Senate primary in Iowa
item.description: Turek defeated progressive opponent Zach Wahls, a state senator who challenged party leadership in Washington. The race in Iowa was the first of a trio of Democratic Senate primaries — followed by Michigan and Minnesota — that will test how much appetite Democratic voters have for candidates skeptical of a more moderate message.

item.title: Supreme Court allows Alabama to use voting map designed to give Republicans an edge
item.description: A lower court found the new map intentionally discriminated on the basis of race and diluted the political power of the state’s Black voters. The high court ruling, though temporary, will allow state officials to implement a 2023 redistricting map that advantages Republicans in six of seven of Alabama’s congressional districts.

item.title: ‘60 Minutes’ correspondent Scott Pelley fired from CBS after confrontation with new executive producer
item.description: In an email to “60 Minutes” staff, the show’s newly hired producer, Nick Bilton, wrote that it had “parted ways” with Pelley. The termination comes after Pelley questioned Bilton’s qualifications during a staff meeting Monday and accused network head Bari Weiss of trying to kill CBS’s iconic newsmagazine, according to people familiar with the situation and an audio recording obtained by The Post.

item.title: Why Trump’s $1.8 billion payout fund led to a rare Republican revolt on Capitol Hill
item.description: The standoff has revealed new strains in President Donald Trump’s relationship with a Congress that has rarely exercised its power to check him, and a shifting political dynamic that could have consequences for the rest of his term.

item.title: What Jill Biden says in her memoir about Melania Trump, the East Wing and that debate
item.description: The former first lady defends her husband’s 2024 decisions and capabilities in a book that also touches on Hunter Biden’s trial, Barron Trump and taking up mah-jongg.

item.title: Trump administration is abandoning $1.8 billion payout fund, acting attorney general says after GOP revolt
item.description: The retreat is a rare instance of congressional Republicans defying the president and prevailing, and comes as the White House seeks to salvage a key part of its aggressive and costly immigration agenda.

item.title: Do these words make you sound smarter? The bias is spreading.
item.description: English speakers love the Romance vocabulary. AI noticed.

item.title: Pentagon hires convicted Jan. 6 rioter for sensitive counterterrorism job
item.description: The Trump administration’s appointment of Elias Irizarry, who was 19 at the time of the Capitol attack, has alarmed some in the Defense Department.

item.title: The Obama Center opens as a time warp to an old political order, columnist Philip Kennicott writes
item.description: Despite controversies surrounding the campus highlighting the history of Barack Obama, the Obama Presidential Center makes a good first impression — even the “Obamalisk” at its center.

item.title: White House correspondents’ dinner rescheduled after first event was cut short by attempted assassination of Trump
item.description: A repeat dinner will be held in July, according to the White House Correspondents’ Association’s president. Details, including security precautions, are scant.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Is friend ‘bean-counting’ them for skipping her pricey fundraiser?
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Is friend ‘bean-counting’ them for skipping her pricey fundraiser?

item.title: Trump signs order creating voluntary process for tech firms to give government early look at powerful AI models
item.description: The order is expected to give officials the chance to brace the economy for security risks. President Donald Trump shelved an earlier proposal following industry backlash.

item.title: After a decade of Trump, who’s leading the 2028 Republican presidential field? Here’s our analysis.
item.description: JD Vance and Marco Rubio top the list, but there’s plenty of room for an anti-Trump candidate.

item.title: Why people stopped having kids
item.description: The fertility crisis is also a religion crisis.

item.title: Trump picks Bill Pulte, the mortgage chief, as acting director of national intelligence
item.description: Pulte will replace Tulsi Gabbard, who said in May that she would leave as DNI this month. Pulte is a staunch political supporter of President Donald Trump. He leads a federal mortgage regulation agency but has no experience with intelligence work.

item.title: An upcoming race in this state in today’s On the Record
item.description: An upcoming race in this state in today’s On the Record

item.title: Payout fund. Presidential contenders. Iran talks. Ukraine. ‘60 Minutes.’ AI job risk. Marriage health benefits. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: These lifestyle changes may help you live longer after a cancer diagnosis
item.description: Adopting certain healthy habits — and cutting out a couple detrimental ones — can improve your outcomes.

item.title: Trump’s payout fund on shaky ground as Republicans push for retreat
item.description: The Justice Department said it would obey a court order pausing the nearly $1.8 billion fund, potentially signaling a broader pullback.

item.title: Spencer Pratt strikes a chord in frustrated Los Angeles
item.description: At a campaign event, the insurgent candidate taps into voter frustration.

item.title: Scott Pelley confronts new ‘60 Minutes’ boss in fiery meeting: ‘We don’t trust you’
item.description: The veteran correspondent demanded answers on fired staff and the new executive producer’s qualifications for the job.

item.title: Pentagon designates press office a classified space, further limiting media access
item.description: The change creates a new barrier compared with previous administrations, under which the office was an open room where reporters could stop by the desks of military public affairs officials without escorts.

item.title: Iran says it is breaking off talks to end war after U.S. and Israeli strikes
item.description: Negotiations were suspended because of Israel’s escalating military attacks in Lebanon and U.S. strikes, an Iranian official told The Post.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: A groom with cold feet is reluctant to walk away
item.description: Carolyn Hax: A groom with cold feet is reluctant to walk away

item.title: In the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential field, who’s leading? Here’s our analysis.
item.description: No candidate has officially jumped in, but governors, senators and former contenders are already shaping the race.

item.title: Loan rules would gut aid for college students whose majors yield low-paying jobs
item.description: Religious colleges, trade schools and others are lobbying to change proposed federal regulations that would deny loans for programs that don’t lead to higher salaries.

item.title: Trump betrayed farmers. Now real signs of anger show.
item.description: Hurt by the war in Iran and war on trade, will farmers still vote red?

item.title: Big words in today’s On the Record
item.description: Big words in today’s On the Record

item.title: Iran strikes. Presidential contenders. Kennedy Center. Trump fund. Cancer hope. Anxiety tips. Picket fences. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: What to know about the vagus nerve — and what the internet gets wrong about it
item.description: This vast network of about 200,000 nerve fibers supports physical and mental well-being in multiple ways, and activating it can help relieve our stress. But experts caution against social media hype about it.

item.title: Trump’s name may come off the Kennedy Center, but he could still destroy it, our columnist writes
item.description: The saga surrounding the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts took another turn with a judge’s ruling against the Trump administration last week. Columnist Philip Kennicott says the decision, in itself, is unlikely to save the institution.

item.title: Blue states want to tax payouts from Trump’s $1.8 billion fund — at 100 percent
item.description: Legislators in Democratic-led states are crafting bills, and Democratic candidates are rallying behind the tactic. The Trump administration has also drawn condemnation from some Republicans over the settlement fund for people who claim they were wrongly investigated by the government.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Mother jumps in the middle of couple’s decision to sleep apart
item.description: The couple sleep apart because it’s practical, but boyfriend’s mother is ready to set her son up on dates.

item.title: Still need to finish this weekend’s On the Record? We’ve got you.
item.description: Play now to finish out your score for the week.
